title: Exploratory Data Analysis of an Auto Imports Dataset
description: Conducted exploratory data analysis on an Auto Imports dataset, including data cleaning, handling of missing values, and detection of outliers.
Produced descriptive statistics and visualisations (histograms, boxplots, scatter plots) alongside correlation analysis to summarise variable distributions and relationships.
Applied statistical methods using SciPy and visualisation libraries to identify trends and support insights into factors influencing automobile pricing.

title: Enhancing Employee Performance Ratings at KiwiLearn
description: Performed exploratory data analysis on employee performance data, including summary statistics, missing-value checks, outlier detection, and visualisations of rating distributions by department.
Applied ANOVA and Tukey’s HSD post-hoc tests to evaluate differences in performance ratings across departments.
Built and evaluated a multiple linear regression model using experience and training hours as predictors, tested regression assumptions, and recommended transformations to address violations.
Provided actionable insights for standardising evaluation criteria and improving professional development opportunities.

title: Maternal Health Risk Analysis
description: Conducted exploratory data analysis on the Maternal Health Risk dataset, including summary statistics, visualisations (histograms, pairplots), and targeted outlier handling.

Developed and tuned Decision Tree classifiers using 10-fold cross-validation; evaluated hyperparameters and model performance with confusion matrices and class-level metrics (precision, recall, F1-score).
Applied statistical and machine learning techniques to assess maternal health risk factors and demonstrated the value of predictive modelling in healthcare contexts.

title: Predicting Bank Marketing Campaign Outcomes
description: Performed exploratory data analysis on the Bank Marketing dataset, including descriptive statistics, visualisations, duplicate/missing-value checks, and class imbalance inspection.
Applied statistical feature selection methods (Chi-square, ANOVA F-tests) to identify influential predictors and prepared data for model development.
Built and evaluated Naïve Bayes, K-Nearest Neighbours, and Multilayer Perceptron models, using cross-validation, hyperparameter tuning, and performance metrics to compare approaches.
Recommended strategies for addressing class imbalance and improving predictive performance through feature engineering and model selection.

title: Comparative Analysis of Text Classification Algorithms
description: Designed and ran experiments comparing Naïve Bayes, Multilayer Perceptron, and SVM classifiers on two text datasets (Depression Reddit and Fake & Real News).
Implemented preprocessing pipelines including stopword removal, lemmatization, and TF-IDF vectorization; applied stratified splits and hyperparameter tuning via GridSearchCV.
Evaluated model performance using precision, recall, and F1-score across multiple preprocessing configurations, documenting comparative strengths and trade-offs.
Provided recommendations on preprocessing strategies and model selection for improving text classification workflows.

title: Statistical Analysis & Modelling
description: Performed statistical analyses including Fisher’s exact test, Pearson’s chi-square, chi-square goodness-of-fit tests, and ANOVA with post-hoc comparisons to evaluate group differences and categorical associations.
Built and assessed regression models, applying multiple linear regression for continuous outcomes and logistic regression with GLMs for binary outcomes, supported by diagnostics and visualisations.
Applied simulation methods to estimate p-values, validated assumptions, and communicated findings through clear statistical reasoning and visual outputs.

title: Forecasting: Time Series Analysis of NZ Economic Indicators
description: Conducted exploratory analysis of economic time series (unemployment, inflation, disposable income), including transformations, decomposition, and autocorrelation checks.
Applied benchmark forecasting methods (mean, naïve, seasonal naïve, drift) and decomposition-based approaches (STL) to generate and compare forecasts.
Built time-series regression models with trend and seasonal components, evaluated assumptions, and compared alternative models for interpretability and accuracy.

title: Forecasting & Time Series Modelling
description: Applied ETS models (simple exponential smoothing, Holt’s linear, Holt–Winters additive and multiplicative) to monthly sales data, evaluated seasonal effects, and assessed transformations.
Performed stationarity analysis with ACF/PACF, applied Box–Cox transformations and differencing, and fitted seasonal ARIMA models with residual diagnostics.
Compared ETS and ARIMA forecasts using accuracy measures and time-series cross-validation, and discussed implications of model choice, forecast intervals, and validation techniques.
